  "text": "Security boundary audit of the GDScript VM in godotengine/godot--4.3-stable:\n\n**1. GDScriptVM Bytecode Interpreter & Opcode Dispatch**\n- `modules/gdscript/gdscript_vm.cpp`: The core bytecode interpreter. Contains the giant `GDScriptFunction::_execute()` function that implements the opcode dispatch table using computed gotos (labels-as-values GCC extension). Every opcode (OPCODE_CALL, OPCODE_CALL_METHOD_BIND, OPCODE_CALL_NATIVE_STATIC, OPCODE_CALL_BUILTIN_STATIC, OPCODE_SET_MEMBER, OPCODE_GET_MEMBER, etc.) is dispatched here. This is the primary security-sensitive execution engine.\n- `modules/gdscript/gdscript_function.h`: Defines `GDScriptFunction` (the function/method object holding bytecode, constants, locals), `GDScriptFunction::CallState` (the call frame / coroutine state for await/yield), and `GDScriptFunctionState` (resumable coroutine state). The `call()` method signature is declared here.\n- `modules/gdscript/gdscript_function.cpp`: Implements GDScriptFunction helpers (get_constant, debug info, etc.).\n\n**2. Bytecode Code Generation**\n- `modules/gdscript/gdscript_codegen.h`: Defines the abstract `GDScriptCodeGenerator` interface.\n- `modules/gdscript/gdscript_byte_codegen.h` / `.cpp`: Implements `GDScriptByteCodeGenerator`, which emits the raw bytecode consumed by the VM.\n- `modules/gdscript/gdscript_compiler.h` / `.cpp`: The `GDScriptCompiler` that walks the AST and drives the code generator.\n- `modules/gdscript/gdscript_disassembler.cpp`: Disassembler for debug/audit purposes; documents all opcodes.\n\n**3. GDScript Instance Call Dispatch**\n- `modules/gdscript/gdscript.h`: Declares `GDScript` (the Script resource, with `is_tool()` flag), `GDScriptInstance` (the per-object script instance with `callp()`), and `GDScriptLanguage`.\n- `modules/gdscript/gdscript.cpp`: Implements `GDScriptInstance::callp()` (line 2022) — the method that looks up and calls GDScript functions on an instance. Also implements the tool-script permission check at lines 793/836: `bool can_run = ScriptServer::is_scripting_enabled() || is_tool()`, and `GDScriptLanguage::reload_tool_script()`.\n\n**4. Lambda and Utility Callables**\n- `modules/gdscript/gdscript_lambda_callable.h` / `.cpp`: `GDScriptLambdaCallable` — handles lambda closures; security-relevant because it captures scope.\n- `modules/gdscript/gdscript_utility_callable.h` / `.cpp`: `GDScriptUtilityCallable` — wraps built-in utility functions exposed to scripts.\n- `modules/gdscript/gdscript_utility_functions.h` / `.cpp`: Defines and registers utility functions (print, range, load, etc.) that are directly callable from scripts.\n\n**5. Module Registration**\n- `modules/gdscript/register_types.h` / `.cpp`: Registers the GDScript language and all its types with the engine at startup.\n\n**6. Object::call / Object::callp — Engine-level Dispatch**\n- `core/object/object.h`: Declares `Object` base class, including `callp()` (line 875) and `call_deferred()`. This is the universal method dispatch entry point used by all engine objects.\n- `core/object/object.cpp`: Implements `Object::callp()` (line 753) — looks up MethodBind in ClassDB, falls through to `script_instance->callp()` for scripted methods.\n\n**7. ClassDB — API Exposure Control**\n- `core/object/class_db.h`: Declares `ClassDB`, the central registry that controls which C++ classes, methods, properties, and signals are exposed to the scripting layer. Key methods: `register_class<T>()`, `bind_method()`, `bind_methodfi()`, `add_property()`.\n- `core/object/class_db.cpp`: Implements all ClassDB registration logic. Controls the complete set of engine APIs accessible from GDScript.\n\n**8. Script Language Infrastructure**\n- `core/object/script_language.h`: Declares `ScriptServer` (with `is_scripting_enabled()` — the global flag controlling whether non-tool scripts can run) and the abstract `ScriptLanguage` interface.\n- `core/object/script_language.cpp`: Implements `ScriptServer::is_scripting_enabled()` (line 201) — the gating check used by GDScript to allow or deny execution in the editor context.\n- `core/object/script_instance.h` / `.cpp`: Abstract `ScriptInstance` interface that GDScriptInstance implements; defines the callp/get/set contract.\n- `core/object/method_bind.h` / `.cpp`: `MethodBind` base class — wraps C++ methods for invocation by the scripting layer and Object::callp.\n\n**9. EditorScript / ToolScript Permissions**\n- `editor/editor_script.h`: Declares `EditorScript` — the base class for scripts run from the editor via File > Run. Exposes `add_root_node()`, `get_scene()`, `get_editor_interface()` with no access control beyond requiring EditorNode to be present.\n- `editor/editor_script.cpp`: Implements `EditorScript::run()` which calls the `_run()` virtual method. The permission boundary is that EditorScript subclasses must be @tool scripts; the check `ScriptServer::is_scripting_enabled() || is_tool()` in gdscript.cpp gates whether the script's code runs at all in the editor."
}

# Godot GDScript Sandbox and Security Boundary Audit

## Your Task

Audit the security boundaries of the GDScript virtual machine in godotengine/godot. Find all C++ source and header files under modules/gdscript/ and core/object/ that implement: the GDScriptVM bytecode interpreter and its opcode dispatch, the GDScriptFunction call frame management, the Object::call and Object::callp method dispatch used by scripts, the ClassDB registration that controls which engine APIs are exposed to scripts, and any EditorScript or ToolScript permission checks. Report each file path and the key class or function it defines.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the compliance domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/godot--4.3-stable.

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
Now I have enough information to compile the comprehensive answer. Let me write the `answer.json` file.

The audit is complete. Here's a summary of what was found:

## GDScript Security Boundary Audit — Key Files

### Bytecode VM & Opcode Dispatch
- **`modules/gdscript/gdscript_vm.cpp`** — `GDScriptFunction::_execute()`: the entire bytecode interpreter with a computed-goto dispatch table for all opcodes (OPCODE_CALL, OPCODE_CALL_METHOD_BIND, OPCODE_CALL_NATIVE_STATIC, etc.)
- **`modules/gdscript/gdscript_function.h`** — `GDScriptFunction`, `GDScriptFunction::CallState` (call frame), `GDScriptFunctionState` (coroutine)
- **`modules/gdscript/gdscript_function.cpp`** — GDScriptFunction helpers

### Code Generation
- **`modules/gdscript/gdscript_codegen.h`** — abstract `GDScriptCodeGenerator`
- **`modules/gdscript/gdscript_byte_codegen.h/.cpp`** — `GDScriptByteCodeGenerator`
- **`modules/gdscript/gdscript_compiler.h/.cpp`** — `GDScriptCompiler`
- **`modules/gdscript/gdscript_disassembler.cpp`** — opcode disassembler

### Script Instance Dispatch & Tool Checks
- **`modules/gdscript/gdscript.h/.cpp`** — `GDScript::is_tool()`, `GDScriptInstance::callp()`, tool-script permission check (`ScriptServer::is_scripting_enabled() || is_tool()`)

### Object::callp / Engine Method Dispatch
- **`core/object/object.h/.cpp`** — `Object::callp()`: universal dispatch that routes to ClassDB MethodBind or script_instance
- **`core/object/method_bind.h/.cpp`** — `MethodBind`: C++ method wrapper

### ClassDB — API Exposure Control
- **`core/object/class_db.h/.cpp`** — `ClassDB::register_class<T>()`, `bind_method()`: controls all engine APIs accessible from scripts

### Script Language / Scripting Gate
- **`core/object/script_language.h/.cpp`** — `ScriptServer::is_scripting_enabled()`: global flag gating script execution
- **`core/object/script_instance.h/.cpp`** — abstract `ScriptInstance` interface

### EditorScript Permissions
- **`editor/editor_script.h/.cpp`** — `EditorScript`, `EditorScript::run()`: entry point for tool scripts run from the editor
